THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT CCSF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS CONTINUED TO MAY 31, 2019 PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(H). THE DECLARATION OF COUNSEL ROBINSON DEMONSTRATES GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE THIS MOTION. OFFICER ENEA, ONE OF THE MAIN WITNESSES IN THIS CASE, HAS YET TO BE DEPOSED. THE FAILURE TO DEPOSE HIM WAS NOT DUE EXCLUSIVELY TO PLAINTIFF'S LACK OF DILIGENCE BUT ALSO BECAUSE OF THE CRIMINAL TRIAL THAT WAS PENDING AGAINST HIM. PLAINTIFF'S SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION IS DUE ON MAY 20, 2019 AND THE SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY IS DUE ON MAY 24, 2019. IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS A FURTHER CONTINUANCE OF THIS MOTION, SHE NEEDS TO OBTAIN A TRIAL CONTINUANCE FROM DEPARTMENT 206 FIRST. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. Atty. Kevin Holl, Esq., of Gordon-Creed Kelley Holl & Sugarman LLP, 415.421.3100, for Defendant Joshua Enea.

Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil - for PLAINTIFF JOANN WALKER.

The matter is called. The Court states that it will clear the virtual ZOOM courtroom to proceed with the in camera hearing. The Court notes that JoAnn Walker is not present. Ms. Walker checks her email and informs the Court that she has not heard from JoAnn Walker. Brief discussions are held.

DISCUSSIONS regarding a continuance of the pending 6/16/21 motion for summary judgment to 8/3/21 are held. Further discussions are held regarding the pitchess motion in Dept. 514. Ms. Stoughton notes that the pending pitchess motion is not in Dept. 514, that it is before Judge DeKreon, and that the matter has stalled.

The Court addresses JoAnn Walker's opposition noting that it is filed in the public record. The Court requests that Ms. Stoughton and Mr. Holl refrain from reviewing Joann Walker's opposition and exhibits. The Court requested that Ms. Price assist Ms. Walker in filing a motion to seal the opposition and exhibits.

At the conclusion of discussions, the Court grants the motion on condition that Ms. Price notify JoAnn Walker of the Court's ruling and the continuance of the 6/16/21 pending motion for summary judgment to 8/3/21 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302.

Ms. Price is to prepare a form of order on the mandatory judicial council form and submit it to the Court for review and signature.

Ms. Stoughton will file an amended notice of hearing regarding the continuance of the 6/16/21 motion for summary judgment to 8/3/21.

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Defendant CCSF's motion for summary adjudication is continued to May 31, 2019 pursuant to CCP 437c(h). The declaration of counsel Robinson demonstrates good cause to continue this motion. Officer Enea, one of the main witnesses in this case, has yet to be deposed. The failure to depose him was not due exclusively to plaintiff's lack of diligence but also because of the criminal trial that was pending against him. Plaintiff's supplemental opposition is due on May 20, 2019 and the supplemental reply is due on May 24, 2019. If Plaintiff seeks a further continuance of this motion, she needs to obtain a trial continuance from Department 206 first.

Order is signed.
